rulings.Case Title: SAMETC, LLC et al VS. TED AND PAULINE ELMS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Prevailing Party And Fixing Attorneys Fees Recoverable As Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 14, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER SAMETC, LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING PREVAILING PARTY AND FIXING ATTORNEYS FEES RECOVERABLE AS COSTS is continued to April 5, 2024. On February 20, 2024 the Court ordered the moving party to file a supplemental brief. The brief has been filed. However, there is no proof of service on file. Instead, the proof of service references "Supplemental Declaration of Counsel Phil Foster Regarding Statute of Pending Appeal." There is no such declaration on file. Moving party to file a proof of service reflecting service of the supplemental brief on March 1, 2024 no later than March 22, 2024. There shall be no further filings in support of or in opposition to this motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARLENE RAMOS ET AL VS. JAY POINDEXTER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 14, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT JAY POINDEXTER, EDUART HOXHA DEMURRER TO 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to address the uncertain and inconsistent allegations, for example, (1) Marlene Ramos is referred to as "Plaintiff" and as "Defendant" in various portions of the operative complaint; (2) it is unclear/confusing/ incomprehensible which Plaintiff is making which allegations; see paragraph 49 and clearly indicate which Plaintiff was "terrified." The examples above are not exhaustive. Plaintiffs must make corrections re: typos/inconsistencies throughout the entire complaint. Plaintiffs shall have 30 days to file an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE CHOW VS. TRI COUNTIES BANK, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition Of Defendant Tri Counties Bank To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 14, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT TRI COUNTIES BANK NOTICE OF PETITION AND PETITION OF DEFENDANT TRI COUNTIES BANK TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS is GRANTED. This action shall proceed in arbitration. Upon completion of the arbitration, parties shall proceed in Court on the equitable relief available under the causes of action for wrongful foreclosure and UCL violations if such relief is warranted based on the arbitration outcome. Parties' contract contains a valid binding arbitration provision that has not been waived and is not unconscionable. The Court considered supplemental briefing submitted by the parties regarding Hasty. The Court further considered additional evidence submitted by the parties. The Court notes that minimal evidence (self-serving declaration only) without any supporting documentation was submitted on the issue of Plaintiff's inability to pay arbitration fees. Based on the evidence submitted (after the Court allowed submission of any evidence parties wished to submit) the Court makes a determination that Plaintiff did not carry its burden regarding inability to pay. Aronow v. Superior Court (2022) 76 Cal.App.5th 865. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
